claim: Harness engineering is the next evolution after prompt engineering and context engineering: orchestrate multiple agent sessions deterministically
actionable: Encode your development workflow as YAML in Archon: mix deterministic steps (validation, context curation) with AI steps (prompts to agents) in loops until tests pass
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: PR acceptance rate jumps from 6.7% (raw model) to ~70% with proper harness (validation, context curation, reliability)
actionable: Build harnesses with validation gates and context control; this is proven to increase production-ready code output by 10x
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: Stripe ships 1,300 AI-only pull requests per week by building custom harnesses around their SDLC
actionable: Reference Stripe Minions blog post; analyze their approach to validation and context curation in harnesses for inspiration
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: Hybrid secret of harnesses: deterministic steps (context curation, validation, test execution) mixed with AI steps (prompts to agents)
actionable: Do NOT rely on agents to remember to run tests or curate context; enforce these as deterministic nodes in your harness YAML
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - How to Build Them

claim: Always run planning and implementation in separate coding sessions to remove bias; use fresh context windows
actionable: In your harness, use separate nodes: one for planning with an agent, then start a new session for implementation
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - How to Build Them

claim: Use cheaper models (Haiku) for classification nodes, reserve expensive models (Opus/Sonnet) for reasoning-heavy steps
actionable: Specify model per node in your Archon YAML; use Haiku for classification/extraction, Sonnet/Opus for complex reasoning to reduce token costs
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: Anthropic's codebase is 40% harness code, signaling that harnesses are fundamental to production agentic systems
actionable: Study Anthropic's harness patterns; this is the direction the industry is moving toward
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: Archon harnesses can be invoked from multiple interfaces: CLI, web UI, GitHub, Slack, Telegram, programmatically
actionable: Build harnesses as YAML once; expose them via Archon CLI and web UI; integrate with your existing tools (GitHub, Slack) using hooks
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: Running 6 parallel Archon workflows shows harnesses enable massive parallelization without manual orchestration
actionable: Design harnesses to be stateless and parallelizable; use Archon to run multiple workflows in parallel across different tasks
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them

claim: Custom slash commands in Claude Code enable reusable compute; when executed by agents in harnesses, they become building blocks
actionable: Build custom slash commands (like /plan, /build); reference them in harness YAML; agents can invoke them naturally
source_title: The Next Evolution of AI Coding Is Harnesses - Here's How to Build Them
